understanding.goal_understanding: Progress through KA59’s levels by positioning every selectable piece onto the goal tiles marked '+'; if a level has multiple '+' tiles, all of them must be occupied by selectables at the same time to trigger WIN and advance until the final level is cleared.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: The currently controlled piece was the one labeled 'P' in the semantic grid and shown in player.position/player.id. MOVE_* attempted to shift that selected piece by one cell step (3 pixels per grid cell) unless blocked by boundaries/walls or interacting objects; some adjacent objects were pushable and would move with the selected piece when pushed, while walls generally blocked movement (with some wall-like boundaries behaving inconsistently, so testing moves was necessary). When multiple selectables existed, CLICK with a target_position in the same pixel-coordinate system switched control to the selectable at that exact position (turning it into the active 'P'), enabling repositioning multiple pieces onto goals.
